title: lomatium simplex var. simplex
intro: Lomatium simplex var. simplex, also known as Western lomatium or Fernleaf biscuitroot, is a perennial wildflower native to the northwestern United States and southwestern Canada. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiviral, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it a potential remedy for various health issues. The medicinal properties of Lomatium simplex var. simplex are attributed to its essential oils, including sesquiterpene lactones and coumarins, which have been shown to exhibit antiviral activity against the common cold and flu viruses, as well as anti-inflammatory effects that may alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. Lomatium simplex var. simplex can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or salve, and has been used to support immune function and reduce inflammation.
